read: A concrete TileLang in-CTA compact dKV reducer now works for a DSv4-like topk512 route by packing random selected groups into bounded compressed-group panels. The retained shape uses group_panel_size=128, per-panel capacity=96, topk workspace=768, block_k=32, block_d=64, token_block=32. It has zero route overflow at S4096, preserves the query-major MFMA producer, accumulates by compact group inside the CTA before global writes, and improves the panelized TileLang path from 23.48 ms / 19.65 ms down to 11.60 ms. It is real kernel progress, but still above the retained 8.0-8.8 ms sparse-atomic wall and still uses diagnostic materialized probs/grad_logits rather than the full fused sparse-MLA/LI backward.

comparison_points.name: panel_padded_random_sw64_k64d32
comparison_points.read: 64-group panels with full 1024-slot padded workspace; correct but too much padded MFMA/input traffic.

comparison_points.name: panel_padded_random_sw128_k64d32
comparison_points.read: 128-group panels reduce route tile count but still pad to all 1024 group slots.

comparison_points.name: panel_capacity_random_cap96_k32d32
comparison_points.read: Capacity packing removes 25 percent of padded selected-slot work with zero overflow; D32 still launches twice as many D blocks as the retained D64 shape.

comparison_points.name: panel_capacity_random_cap96_k32d64_tb64
comparison_points.read: Token block 64 halves compact partial rows to 128 MiB but is slightly slower than token block 32, so partial write volume is no longer the sole wall.

rejected_or_unretained_variants.reason: The unpanelized active table grows too large for TileLang layout inference.
K40 is not MFMA-divisible by 16; K16 hits a TileLang layout divide-by-zero path.
Fast at S128 but drops about 3.39 percent of selected pairs, so it is not a valid topk512 route.
TileLang chooses unsupported warp partitions for these MFMA tile shapes under the tested thread counts.
Correct at S128 but slower than block_d=64.
Correct at S128 but slower than 256 threads.

next_kernel_work: Fuse target_probs/grad_logits production into this TileLang route instead of reading materialized [S,K,H] diagnostic inputs.
Reduce the 256 MiB compact partial stream, likely with a second in-kernel selected-panel merge or by writing directly into LI/compressor dKV when ownership is unambiguous.
If TileLang cannot express the needed segmented merge without route padding, move this retained contract to HIP/rocWMMA/CK while keeping the group_panel_size=128/capacity=96 route layout.
